question_text: A uniform metre ruler is balanced at its centre (50 cm mark). A 2 N weight is hung from the 20 cm mark. Where must a 5 N weight be hung to balance the ruler?
explanation: Using the principle of moments: clockwise moments = anticlockwise moments. The 2 N weight creates a moment of 2 N × 30 cm = 60 N·cm on one side. The 5 N weight must create the same moment on the other side: 5 N × distance = 60 N·cm, so distance = 12 cm from the pivot. This puts it at the 50 − 18 = 32 cm mark.
